rulings.Case Title: 756 PAGE STRET, LLC. VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: REAL PROPERTY/ HOUSING COURT LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR JULY 8, 2020 LINE 1. PETITIONER 756 PAGE STREET's, LLC. Motion For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Petitioner's Writ for Administrative Mandamus is GRANTED. Administrative Law Judge abused discretion by dismissing the action with prejudice. The record shows that Petitioner took reasonable steps to inform the Rent Board of his condition before the hearing by leaving a voicemail. This information was in the Rent Board's possession at the time of the dismissal, and became known to the Administrative Law Judge 5 minutes after oral dismissal and two weeks before the written order . The Order dismissing the Petition mentions the fact that the Petitioner failed to provide any verification of his illness. Nowhere in the record does it show that Petitioner was informed that verification was required or what kind of verification was required for the sudden onset of the illness to be provided on the morning of the hearing by 9:20 a.m. Administrative Law Judge had three less drastic measures to chose from and was reasonably apprised of the fact that Petitioner did not abandon the action by virtue of a representative/witness being present at the hearing. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Rulings: REAL PROPERTY/ HOUSING COURT LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR JULY 8, 2020 LINE 3. Order To Show Cause READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. OSC RE: Why Plaintiff Should not Be Required to Respond to Additional Interrogatories. Plaintiff is not required to respond to Special Interrogatories, Set Two. 35 interrogatories in Set Two are substantially the same as those included in the set of 212 subject to Protective Order. Request for Sanctions is denied as no authority is cited for the proposition that sanctions may or should be awarded on this Order to Show Cause. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: STELLA DORDELLY ET AL VS. JESAMINE MAK ET AL
rulings.Rulings: REAL PROPERTY/ HOUSING COURT LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR JULY 8, 2020 LINE 6. DEFENDANT JESAMINE MAK and DICK MAK's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendants' Demurrer to Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint is OVERRULED. Plaintiffs sufficiently allege a cause of action for each of the three causes of action in the First Amended Complaint. Defendants must file an Answer within ten days pursuant to California Rules of Court, rule 3.1320(j). Defendants' request for $4,084 in attorney's fees and costs associated with the Demurrer to Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint is DENIED as Defendants did not file a separate motion under Code of Civil Procedure section 128.5. Additionally, Defendants did not comply with the twenty-one day safe harbor period as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 128.5. =(501/CFH)
